heading: Advances for payments for compliance with foreign laws, rent in foreign countries, tuition, public utility services, and pay and supplies of armed forces of friendly foreign countries
label: 2396 Advances for payments for compliance with foreign laws, rent in foreign countries, tuition, public utility services, and pay and supplies of armed forces of friendly foreign countries
notes: Historical and Revision Notes1982 . 97–258)Act (Pub. LRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)2395(a)31:529i., .July 13, 1955, ch. 358, § 60269 Stat. 3142395(b)31:529j., , .Oct. 19, 1965Pub. L. 89–26579 Stat. 989In subsection (a), the words “On and after ” are omitted as executed. The words “An advance” are substituted for “ shall not apply in the case of payments” because of the restatement.July 13, 1955section 529 of this titleIn subsection (b), the words “armed force of the United States” are substituted for “Army, Navy, Air Force, Marine Corps, or Coast Guard” because of 10:101(4) and to avoid confusion with the phrase “armed force of a friendly foreign country”.In subsection (b)(1), before clause (A), the words “the Secretary of Transportation with respect to the Coast Guard when it is not operating as a service in the Navy” are substituted for “the Secretary of the Treasury in their respective areas of responsibility” because of 14:3 and 49:1655(b)(1) and (2). The words “disbursing official” are substituted for “cashiers, disbursing officers” for consistency with other titles of the United States Code and to eliminate unnecessary words.

(b)(2)(C).Pub. L. 97–258

Amendments2002—Subsec. (b)(1). substituted “of Homeland Security” for “of Transportation” in introductory provisions.Pub. (b)(2)(C). substituted “any other” for “another”.Pub. L. 97–295

children.text: compliance with laws and ministerial regulations of a foreign country;
rent in a foreign country for periods of time determined by local custom;

children.label: A
children.text: pay and allowances to members of the armed force of that country; and

children.label: A
children.text: requiring reimbursement to the United States for amounts advanced;

children.label: B
children.text: requiring the appropriate authority of the country to advance amounts reciprocally to members of the armed forces of the United States; and

children.label: C
children.text: containing any other provision the President considers necessary to carry out this subsection and to safeguard the interests of the United States.
